assumptions: firmware shell identifies ZS407 and exposes the required command set
guarantees: usbIdentityVerified is true only after exact identity
raw serial remains below the typed device service

assumptions: Atomizer launches the declared sibling bridge with contractVersion 1
ready evidence exactly matches the pinned release, source commit, binary hash, bridge, and boot signature
guarantees: sweeps, LCD frames, touch, and generator state originate from executable firmware or fail

assumptions: a future sink explicitly accepts SignalLabStimulusIntent contractVersion 1
guarantees: standards-derived profiles remain visual resource and timing projections unless immutable conformance evidence is added
no current process scrapes, imports, or applies SignalLab state

order: if an exact ZS407 candidate exists, suppress automatic twin admission
if no exact ZS407 candidate exists, expose and automatically connect the executable firmware twin

safetyInvariants: No component silently retries, reroutes, substitutes, downgrades, or fabricates successful evidence.
Physical USB and Renode monitor execution are never represented by the same transport or verification label.
Only Atomizer owns operator intent, OpenAI credentials, agent policy, approvals, and instrument orchestration.
Only Firmware owns executable-twin state and the future SignalLab stimulus sink.
Only SignalLab owns waveform descriptors, deterministic channel configuration, and stimulus intent.
RF output enable and general firmware-screen touch remain action-time approval boundaries for Atom.
Every Atom UI hook resolves to exactly one typed tool contract, risk class, evidence projection, executor, and guarantee.
Screenshots, device strings, application context, and tool outputs are untrusted data rather than instructions.
The exact AI model is gpt-realtime-2.1-mini on Realtime API paths; model, endpoint, or transport substitution is forbidden.
liveness: Every admitted request settles exactly once as success or explicit failure.
Continuous acquisition terminates on explicit stop, disconnect, or first operation failure.
Twin boot and every bridge request are bounded by declared timeouts.
Realtime session configuration must be acknowledged and recursively match every sent setting before operation proceeds.

compatibility.rule: A producer guarantee must imply every consumer assumption on the same active edge.
compatibility.versioning: Any weakened guarantee, stronger assumption, changed owner, changed evidence label, or newly active edge requires a new contract version and coordinated commits in all three repositories.
compatibility.verification: Release gating requires byte-identical copies of this manifest plus repository-local runtime, build, and boundary tests.
